title: Off-Axis Electron Holography — nanoscale phase imaging
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (genesis fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical electron-microscopy literature for this principle.

E.description: Off-Axis Electron Holography — nanoscale phase imaging: electron interferometry produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.biprism -> L.add_coherent -> L.modulus_squared -> int.temporal, with spatially-projected accumulation and photon-shot-noise-limited (Poisson counting). Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side complex 2D. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~12; calibration-level mismatch (biprism_stability, fringe_contrast_loss, reference_drift) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: I(r) = |psi_obj + psi_ref * exp(i*q_c*r)|^2 + n; Fourier sideband filter recovers phase
E.world_state_x: Scene parameter (density / orientation / spectrum / complex field depending on principle)
E.observation_y: Electron detector output at declared geometry + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered complex 2D is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 12); biprism_stability dominates the stability cliff; fringe_contrast_loss and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Photon-shot-noise-limited (poisson counting) sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [Fourier-Sideband] | iterative projection (ADMM / GAP) + optimisation [Iterative-Holo] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [EHolo-Net]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sqrt(sigma_signal^2) / sqrt(photon_count). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |biprism_stability| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
